title: Pitcairnia theae

The Bromeliaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Pitcairnia theae belonging to the subfamily Pitcairnioideae. This subfamily is a group of tropical plants native to Central and South America, with characteristics that include strap-like leaves and brightly colored flowers. The specific classification of Pitcairnia theae also includes its genus and species classification.

intro: Pitcairnia theae, also known as the tea bromeliad, is a type of epiphytic plant belonging to the family Bromeliaceae. This plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its potential health benefits. The tea bromeliad has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
